helpful: Provisioned questions-presented.txt / petition.txt / brief-in-opposition.txt — the QP text plus the SG's BIO carried most of the predictive signal
Statpack relist-count and originating-circuit cuts for anchoring
One CourtListener opinion search to confirm the no-circuit-split claim in the BIO
gaps: Statpack has no salience-band table in this build, though the prompt tells predictors to prefer it
No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions — the strongest docket signal here had no in-repo base rate; I had to rely on general knowledge (~8-10% grant for CFR'd petitions)
fedcourts query citation matching returned 0 rows even for 601 U.S. 416; citation-overlap retrieval seems to have little coverage for recent SCOTUS petition dockets
notes: petition.txt was truncated (117 pages, flag truncated=true) but the QPs, statement, and core cert-stage argument were within the provisioned portion, so truncation did not materially degrade the prediction.
